sections.paragraph:
ἐπιτυγχάνω fut. 3 sg. ἐπιτεύξεται Pr 12:27; 2 aor. ἐπέτυχον (s. τυγχάνω; Pre-Socr., Hdt.+; ins, pap, LXX, pseudepigr.; Jos., Ant. 5, 288; Philo; Just.; Ath., R. 57, 16 al.) to be successful in achieving or gaining what one seeks, obtain, attain to, reach w. gen. of what is reached (Aristoph., Plut. 245; Thu. 3, 3, 3; BGU 113, 3; 522, 8; Jos., Ant. 18, 279) of a thing (GrBar 4:13 ὀργῆς θεοῦ; ApcEsdr 6:22 στεφάνου; Appian, Iber. 48 §200 εἰρήνης) Hm 10, 2, 4; 2 Cl 5:6. τῆς ἐπαγγελίας Hb 6:15; cp. 11:33. τοῦ κλήρου ITr 12:3. χάριτος IRo 1:2a.—θεοῦ attain to God, specif. Ignatian expr., meant to designate martyrdom as a direct way to God: IEph 12:2; IMg 14; ITr 12:2; 13:3; IRo 1:2; 2:1; 4:1; 9:2; ISm 11:1; IPol 2:3; 7:1. Also ἐ. Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ IRo 5:3. Without obj. IPhld 5:1.—W. acc. obtain (X., Hell. 4, 5, 19; 4, 8, 21; 6, 3, 16; UPZ 41, 26 [161/160 b.c.] πᾶν ἐπιτυγχάνειν; TestSol 9:3 προδότην) Ro 11:7. οὐδὲν τ. αἰτημάτων none of the things requested Hm 9:5.—W. inf. foll. (Lucian, Necyom. 6; Jos., Bell. 1, 302; Just.) IRo 1:1. Abs. (Pla., Men., 97c; SIG 736, 79 [92 b.c.]) Ac 13:29 D; Js 4:2; IEph 1:2; IRo 8:3.—M-M. Sv.

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

aor. aor. = aorist

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

pseudepigr. pseudepigr. = pseudepigraphon, pseudepigrapha

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

gen. gen. = genitive

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Hm Hm = Mandates

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

IRo IRo = Ignatius to the Romans—List 1

specif. specif. = specifically

expr. expr. = expression

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

IMg IMg = Ignatius to the Magnesians—List 1

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

IPol IPol = Ignatius to Polycarp—List 1

obj. obj. = object, objective

IPhld IPhld = Ignatius to the Philadelphians—List 1

acc. acc. = accusative

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

inf. inf. = infinitive

foll. foll. = followed, following

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Men. Men. = De Mensibus

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 385
